key_quote: The algebraic 'proof' (algebraic_proof.py) tests f(x)==f(x) — perturbing quality scores that are never passed to sinkhorn_divergence, making the 8.93e-17 error a floating-point determinism artifact, not evidence of a mathematical property
rationale: The core insight — Sinkhorn divergence's embedding-only dependence yielding exact quasi-linearity — is acknowledged as genuine and elegant. But the execution has critical failures: tautological proof, broken DPP baseline (identical to TopQuality), empirically falsified composition theorem bounds (0.606 vs claimed 0.312), zero sound verification, and synthetic-only evaluation. If implementation were perfect, reviewers would not say 'not novel'. This is Category B: novel idea, deeply broken execution.
